notes: Structured events derived from the free-text 'notes' fields in data/publisher_imprints.json. Event types: founded, merger_formed (founded via merger of predecessors), renamed, merged_with, acquired_by, sold_to, defunct. Each event quotes its source phrase as 'evidence' for auditability. External actors (e.g. Bertelsmann, RCA) appear as nodes in the consolidation graph even though they have no entry in publisher_imprints.json.

McGraw-Hill Book Company.evidence: Founded 1909 by merger of McGraw Publishing and Hill Publishing.

Doubleday & Company.evidence: Doubleday, Doran (1927-1946) after merging with George H. Doran Company.

D. C. Heath and Company.evidence: U.S. educational publisher founded by Daniel Collamore Heath.
sold to Houghton Mifflin and absorbed.

Oxford University Press.evidence: Department of the University of Oxford.

Random House.evidence: Founded 1927 by Bennett Cerf and Donald Klopfer.
in 2013 merged with Penguin to form Penguin Random House.

Alfred A. Knopf.evidence: Founded by Alfred A. Knopf Sr. and Blanche Knopf.

Macmillan Publishing Company.evidence: U.S. arm of the British Macmillan Publishers.
before becoming part of Holtzbrinck in 1994.

Harper & Brothers.evidence: Founded as J. & J. Harper in 1817.
Merged with Row, Peterson and Company in 1962 to become Harper & Row.
merged with William Collins in 1989 to form HarperCollins.

Charles Scribner's Sons.evidence: subsequently passed to Simon & Schuster in 1994.

Houghton Mifflin Company.evidence: Founded 1880 as Houghton, Mifflin and Company.

The Viking Press.evidence: Founded by Harold Guinzburg and George Oppenheimer in 1925.
Merged with Penguin in 1975 to form Viking Penguin.
Merged with Penguin in 1975 to form Viking Penguin; continues as an imprint.

Henry Holt and Company.evidence: Merged with Rinehart and Company and the John C. Winston Company in 1960 to form Holt, Rinehart and Winston.
to form Holt, Rinehart and Winston.
restored the Henry Holt and Company name in 1985 for the trade arm.

Harcourt, Brace and Company.evidence: Founded 1919 by Alfred Harcourt, Donald Brace, and (briefly) Will D. Howe as Harcourt, Brace and Howe.
Renamed Harcourt, Brace & World after merging with World Book in 1960.
Harcourt Brace Jovanovich after a 1970 acquisition.

John Wiley & Sons.evidence: Founded by Charles Wiley in New York.

W. W. Norton & Company.evidence: Founded 1923 by William Warder Norton and Mary Dows Herter Norton.
Acquired by Norton in 1974; originally Boni & Liveright (1917-1933). Carries the historical Liveright back-catalogue.

J. B. Lippincott Company.evidence: Founded 1836 by Joshua B. Lippincott in Philadelphia.
Trade publishing operations acquired by Harper & Row in 1978.

Simon & Schuster.evidence: Founded 1924 by Richard L. Simon and M. Lincoln Schuster.

Thomas Y. Crowell Company.evidence: Merged with P. F. Collier and Son to form Crowell-Collier Publishing Company in 1939.
to form Crowell-Collier Publishing Company in 1939.
The Crowell name persisted on trade titles until folded into Harper & Row in 1980.

Bobbs-Merrill Company.evidence: Acquired by Howard W. Sams in 1958.
sold to Macmillan in 1985 and dissolved.

Funk & Wagnalls.evidence: Founded 1877 by Isaac Kaufmann Funk and Adam Willis Wagnalls.

D. Appleton-Century Company.evidence: Formed 1933 by merger of D. Appleton & Company (1825-1933) and The Century Company (1881-1933).
Became Appleton-Century-Crofts in 1948 after acquiring F. S. Crofts & Co.
after acquiring F. S. Crofts & Co.

Farrar, Straus and Giroux.evidence: Farrar, Straus and Giroux 1964 when Roger W. Straus Jr. made Robert Giroux a partner.
Acquired by Farrar, Straus and Giroux in 1971.

Atheneum Publishers.evidence: Founded 1959 by Alfred A. Knopf Jr., Simon Michael Bessie, and Hiram Haydn.

Pantheon Books.evidence: Founded 1942 in New York by Kurt and Helen Wolff and Jacques Schiffrin.
Operated independently until 1961, when it was acquired by Random House.

Faber and Faber.evidence: British publisher founded 1929 from the reorganization of Faber and Gwyer (1925-1929).

Jonathan Cape.evidence: British publisher founded 1921 by Jonathan Cape and Wren Howard.
acquired by Random House UK in 1987.

Penguin Books.evidence: Founded 1935 by Allen Lane as a paperback imprint of The Bodley Head.
merged with Random House in 2013 to form Penguin Random House.

Hamish Hamilton.evidence: British trade publisher founded 1931 by Jamie Hamilton.

Prentice-Hall.evidence: U.S. educational publisher founded 1913 by Charles Gerstenberg and Richard Ettinger.

Reinhold Publishing Corporation.evidence: merged with Van Nostrand to form Van Nostrand Reinhold.

Dodd, Mead and Company.evidence: Closed in 1990 after sale to a holding company.

E. P. Dutton.evidence: Founded 1864 in Boston by Edward Payson Dutton.

New American Library.evidence: Spun off in 1948 from Penguin Books' U.S. arm by Kurt Enoch and Victor Weybright.
Acquired by the Times Mirror Company in 1960.

Crown Publishers.evidence: Founded 1933 by Nat Wartels and Robert Simon as a remainder house.

William Heinemann.evidence: British trade publisher founded 1890 by William Heinemann.

Methuen Publishing.evidence: British publisher founded 1889 by Algernon Methuen.
Acquired by Associated Book Publishers in 1968.

George Allen & Unwin.evidence: British publisher founded 1914 when Stanley Unwin acquired George Allen & Co.
Merged with Bell & Hyman in 1990 to form Unwin Hyman.

Eyre & Spottiswoode.evidence: dissolved as a trade imprint by 1990.

Cassell.evidence: British publisher founded 1848 by John Cassell.
then to Orion Publishing Group in 1998.

Boni & Liveright.evidence: Founded 1917 by Albert Boni and Horace Liveright in New York.
Renamed Horace Liveright, Inc. in 1928 after Liveright bought out Boni's remaining interest.

The Newcomen Society in North America.evidence: American branch (founded 1923) of the British Newcomen Society.

Vanguard Press.evidence: U.S. trade publisher founded 1926 with a Charles Garland endowment.

The Bodley Head.evidence: British publisher founded 1887 by John Lane and Elkin Mathews.

Routledge & Kegan Paul.evidence: Formed 1947 by merger of George Routledge & Sons (founded 1836) and Kegan Paul, Trench, Trubner & Co. (founded 1877).
Merged into Routledge as part of the Taylor & Francis Group in 1985.

The Free Press.evidence: Founded 1947 by Jeremiah Kaplan in Glencoe, Illinois.

Ballantine Books.evidence: Founded 1952 by Ian and Betty Ballantine after Ian left Bantam Books.

Bantam Books.evidence: Founded 1945 by Walter B. Pitkin Jr., Sidney B. Kramer, and Ian Ballantine.

Avon Books.evidence: Founded 1941 by Joseph Meyers and Edna Williams Meyers as Avon Pocket-Size Books.
Acquired by the Hearst Corporation in 1959.

Fawcett Publications.evidence: Founded 1919 by Wilford Hamilton Fawcett (Captain Billy) as a magazine publisher.
book imprints sold to Random House in 1982.

Dell Publishing.evidence: Founded 1921 by George T. Delacorte Jr. as a magazine publisher.

Grosset & Dunlap.evidence: Founded 1898 by Alexander Grosset and George T. Dunlap.

New Directions Publishing.evidence: Founded 1936 by James Laughlin in Norfolk, Connecticut.

Harvard University Press.evidence: Founded 1913. The publishing arm of Harvard University.

Yale University Press.evidence: Founded 1908. The publishing arm of Yale University.

University of Chicago Press.evidence: Founded 1891 as part of the new University of Chicago.

Princeton University Press.evidence: Founded 1905. The publishing arm of Princeton University.

Columbia University Press.evidence: Founded 1893. The publishing arm of Columbia University.

MIT Press.evidence: Began 1932 as the Technology Press of MIT.
renamed The MIT Press in 1962 after taking over its own distribution.

Western Publishing.evidence: Founded 1907 in Racine, Wisconsin, as Western Printing and Lithographing.
Acquired by Mattel in 1996 and split up.

Time-Life Books.evidence: Founded 1961 as the book-publishing division of Time Inc.

World Book, Inc..evidence: World Book Encyclopedia originated 1917 with the Hanson-Roach-Fowler Company.

W. B. Saunders Company.evidence: Founded 1888 by Walter Burns Saunders in Philadelphia.
sold to Holt, Rinehart and Winston in 1980.
then to Harcourt Brace Jovanovich in 1986.

Beacon Press.evidence: Founded 1854 by the American Unitarian Association.

G. Schirmer, Inc..evidence: Founded 1861 by Gustav Schirmer in New York.
sold to Music Sales Group in 1986.

Arnoldo Mondadori Editore.evidence: Italy's largest book publisher, founded 1907 by Arnoldo Mondadori in Ostiglia.

predecessors: Compañía Anónima de Librería y Publicaciones Españolas
Espasa-Calpe.evidence: Spanish reference and trade publisher formed 1925 by merger of Editorial Espasa (Barcelona) and Compañía Anónima de Librería y Publicaciones Españolas (Madrid).

The Limited Editions Club.evidence: Founded 1929 by George Macy as a subscription book club.

Quadrangle Books.evidence: Founded 1958 in Chicago by Melvin Brisk and Ivan von Auw.
Acquired by The New York Times Company in 1969.

Quadrangle Books.to: Quadrangle/The New York Times Book Co.
Quadrangle Books.evidence: becoming Quadrangle/The New York Times Book Co.

Abingdon Press.evidence: the Abingdon Press name dates from 1908.

Westminster Press.evidence: Publishing arm of the Presbyterian Church (U.S.A.) and its predecessors.
Merged with the John Knox Press in 1988 to form Westminster John Knox Press.
to form Westminster John Knox Press.

Wm. B. Eerdmans Publishing Co..evidence: Founded 1911 by William B. Eerdmans in Grand Rapids, Michigan.

Sheed & Ward.evidence: Roman Catholic publisher founded 1926 in London by Frank Sheed and Maisie Ward.

Éditions Gallimard.evidence: founded 1911 by Gaston Gallimard, Jean Schlumberger, and André Gide.

Springer-Verlag.evidence: Founded 1842 in Berlin by Julius Springer.
Acquired by Kluwer in 2003 forming Springer Science+Business Media.
merged with Macmillan Science & Education in 2015 to form Springer Nature.

Academic Press.evidence: Founded 1942 in New York by Kurt Jacoby and Walter J. Johnson.
Acquired by Harcourt Brace Jovanovich in 1969.

Flammarion.evidence: French general-interest publisher founded 1875 by Ernest Flammarion.

Hachette Livre.evidence: French publishing group founded 1826 by Louis Hachette.

Éditions Albin Michel.evidence: French independent literary publisher founded 1900 by Albin Michel in Paris.

Éditions Bernard Grasset.evidence: French literary publisher founded 1907 by Bernard Grasset.

Éditions Robert Laffont.evidence: French publisher founded 1941 by Robert Laffont in Marseille.

La Table Ronde.evidence: French literary publisher founded 1944 by Roland Laudenbach.

William Morrow and Company.evidence: Founded 1926 by William Morrow in New York.

Rinehart & Company.evidence: Founded 1929 by Stanley M. Rinehart Jr., Frederick R. Rinehart, and John C. Farrar as Farrar & Rinehart.
After Farrar left in 1946 to start Farrar, Straus & Company, it became Rinehart & Company.
Merged with Henry Holt and John C. Winston Company in 1960 to form Holt, Rinehart and Winston.

Duell, Sloan and Pearce.evidence: Founded 1939 by Charles Halliwell Duell, Samuel Sloan, and Charles Pearce.

Pergamon Press.evidence: British scientific and technical publisher founded 1948 by Robert Maxwell.
Acquired by Elsevier in 1991 after Maxwell's death.

William Collins, Sons.evidence: British publisher founded 1819 in Glasgow by William Collins.
merged with Harper & Row to form HarperCollins.

Thames & Hudson.evidence: British illustrated-books publisher founded 1949 by Walter Neurath and Eva Feuchtwang.

University of California Press.evidence: Publishing arm of the University of California, founded 1893.

University of Minnesota Press.evidence: Publishing arm of the University of Minnesota.

Johns Hopkins University Press.evidence: The oldest continuously operating university press in the United States.

Victor Gollancz Ltd.evidence: British publisher founded 1927 by Victor Gollancz.

J. M. Dent & Sons.evidence: British publisher founded 1888 by Joseph Malaby Dent.

McClelland and Stewart.evidence: founded 1906 in Toronto by John McClelland and Frederick Goodchild.
Sold 75% interest to Random House Canada in 2000; acquired wholly in 2011.

Literary Guild of America.evidence: U.S. subscription book club founded 1927 by Samuel W. Craig and Harold Guinzburg.

Julian Press.evidence: U.S. publisher founded 1949 by Arthur Ceppos in New York.

American Society for Testing and Materia.to: American Society for Testing and Materials

Dunod.evidence: French scientific and technical publisher; traces to the 1791 Mme. Vve. Courcier mathematics imprint.
